content.text: Keys Mode transforms the number pads into a chromatic or scale-locked keyboard, allowing you to play melodies and chords with your samples. This is perfect for pitched instruments like synths, pianos, and bass sounds.
Press the KEYS button to enter keyboard mode. The 12 pads (0-9, +, -) now play the currently selected sound at different pitches across one octave.
By default, Keys Mode is chromatic - each pad plays a semitone higher than the previous, covering a full octave. The pad layout follows a piano-like arrangement.
While in Keys Mode, use the +/- buttons (when not playing) or a key combination to shift the octave up or down. This extends your range beyond the 12 pads.
The EP-133 includes several scale modes that constrain notes to a specific musical scale. This makes it easier to play in key without hitting wrong notes.

items: Blues - Minor pentatonic with blue note

content.text: Set the root note (key) for scale modes. This determines which note is the tonic. For example, setting root to 'G' with Minor scale gives you G minor.
Keys Mode works with the sequencer. Enable RECORD and play melodies on the pads - your performance is recorded with the correct pitches. This is how you create melodic sequences.

content.title: Melody Workflow
content.text: 1. Sample or load a melodic sound (synth, piano, etc.). 2. Enter Keys Mode. 3. Select a scale and root note. 4. Record your melody into the sequencer.
Press multiple pads simultaneously to play chords. The EP-133's polyphony (16 mono / 12 stereo voices) allows for rich chord voicings. Scale mode helps ensure your chords are in key.
Press KEYS again or MAIN to exit Keys Mode and return to normal pad triggering where each pad plays a different sound.
